chunks.text: Words have potential.
A single word can destroy a person, or save one.
The problem of bullying has long been a concern of society.
In schools, at workplaces, and on the internet.
Not only visible violence, but also the violence of words carries serious implication.
Children are particularly vulnerable.
" Ugly.
" " Stupid.
" " Go die.
" Words thrown casually can cut deeply into a child's heart.
Slander on social media intensifies through anonymity.
Under malice sent daily by invisible senders
many young people are pushed to the edge of their mental limits.
Some reach suicide.
" Words have consequence.
" We are recently rediscovering this truth.
What was meant as a light joke may hurt the other like a blade.
But words also carry the power to heal.
" Thank you.
" " It's okay.
" " You are not alone.
" Such words can lift someone who is about to collapse.
Indeed, words are both weapon and medicine.
Which one they become depends on the choice of each one of us.
Today, pay attention to the words you speak.
What can you offer to lighten someone's heart?
